full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Agreed to a Lepanto alliance with Austria, including a mutual DMZ in Tyrolia, Venice, Trieste, and the Adriatic Sea. Austria is moving F TRI - ALB, A BUD - SER, and A VIE - BUD. Established DMZs with France (Piedmont, Gulf of Lyon, Tyrrhenian Sea) and Germany (Tyrolia). Maintained friendly relations with England and Russia for potential future coordination. Deceived Turkey by agreeing to a demilitarized Eastern Med while actually preparing the Lepanto.
Intent: Execute the standard Lepanto opening as agreed with Austria: F NAP - ION, A ROM - APU, and A VEN Hold. This respects the DMZs with Austria, France, and Germany while setting up for a Fall convoy to Tunis and future action against Turkey.

[S1901M] The Spring 1901 phase went exactly as planned. The Lepanto alliance with Austria is in full effect; Austria executed their agreed moves (F TRI - ALB, A BUD - SER) and respected our DMZs in Venice, Trieste, the Adriatic, and Tyrolia. France and Germany also honored our DMZ agreements, leaving my borders completely secure. 

Turkey executed a standard opening (F ANK - BLA, A CON - BUL), while Russia moved F SEV - RUM, avoiding a bounce in the Black Sea. This suggests a possible non-aggression pact between Russia and Turkey, making our Lepanto even more crucial to contain them.

My strategic position is excellent. I am perfectly positioned to convoy A APU to Tunis via F ION in the Fall. Austria has proven trustworthy, solidifying our alliance. My next steps are to secure Tunis, build F NAP in the Winter, and prepare to move F ION to the Eastern Mediterranean in 1902 to pressure Turkey.

[F1901M] Solidified the Lepanto alliance with Austria, who will take GRE while I convoy to TUN. Secured an anti-Turkish alliance with Russia, who is furious about Turkey taking the Black Sea; we will coordinate attacks in 1902. Maintained strong DMZ agreements with France (Piedmont, GoL, TYS) and Germany (Tyrolia). Successfully deceived Turkey into believing the Eastern Med DMZ is secure and our relations are peaceful.
Intent: Execute the agreed Fall Lepanto moves: F ION C A APU - TUN, A APU - TUN, and A VEN Hold. This secures Tunis, respects all DMZs with France, Germany, and Austria, and perfectly sets up a Winter build of F NAP for the 1902 offensive against Turkey.

[F1901M] The Fall 1901 phase unfolded flawlessly. The Lepanto alliance with Austria remains rock solid; they successfully took Greece while I convoyed my army to Tunis. Crucially, the anticipated conflict between Russia and Turkey erupted in the east. Turkey attempted to force Romania with Black Sea support, but Russia successfully defended it with a strong three-unit wall. This Turkish aggression cements Russia as a vital ally and ensures Turkey is distracted on multiple fronts. 

My borders with France and Germany remain secure, as both respected our DMZs while focusing on the west (France even supported England into Belgium). 

My strategic position is exceptional. I have secured my fourth center (Tunis) and will build F NAP this Winter. In 1902, I will move F ION to the Eastern Mediterranean and F NAP to ION, springing the Lepanto trap on a Turkey that is already bogged down fighting Russia.

[S1902M] Solidified the Lepanto alliance with Austria and Russia to crush Turkey. Austria is moving F TRI-ALB, F GRE-AEG, and A SER-BUL. Russia is attacking the Black Sea and Armenia. Successfully deceived Turkey into believing I am moving F NAP-TYS and holding F ION to counter France, prompting Turkey to move F SMY-CON and leave the East Med clear. Maintained DMZs with France (Piedmont, GoL, TYS) and Germany (Tyrolia). England and Germany warned that France is moving F BRE-MAO, so I must monitor my western flank.
Intent: Execute the Spring 1902 Lepanto moves: F ION - EAS and F NAP - ION. Hold A VEN to respect DMZs with Austria, Germany, and France. Hold A TUN. This springs the trap on Turkey while they are distracted by Austria and Russia.

[S1902M] The Lepanto trap has been perfectly executed! My fleets successfully moved to the Eastern Mediterranean and the Ionian Sea, while Austria positioned fleets in the Aegean and Albania. Turkey was completely caught off guard, wasting their moves attempting to force Romania while jamming their own units (F SMY and A CON bounced). Russia held strong in Romania, cementing our three-way anti-Turkish alliance. 

Austria continues to prove themselves as a highly trustworthy and competent ally. Our coordination is flawless. In the west, France moved F POR to MAO, which warrants monitoring, but they are heavily engaged with Germany (who took Burgundy) and England, keeping my western flank relatively safe. 

For Fall 1902, my primary objective is to coordinate with Austria to convoy A TUN to Syria or directly assault Smyrna. I must maintain my secure borders in Venice and Piedmont while delivering a crippling blow to Turkey.

[F1902M] Austria and Russia are fully coordinated to attack Turkey; Austria will order A SER - BUL supported by F AEG and Russia's F RUM, while Austria also moves F ALB - GRE. Russia confirmed Turkey plans to order A BUL - SER, walking into the trap. I have agreed with Austria and Russia to convoy A TUN to SYR. Germany successfully invaded Burgundy and encouraged me to attack France, but I declined for now to focus east, though Germany warned me to watch Austria's rapid growth. France requested a distraction against Germany, but I confirmed I will maintain our southern DMZs. Turkey pleaded for me to stop, warning of an Austrian juggernaut; I feigned that I would just 'hold' in EAS to see how things develop.
Intent: Execute the Lepanto convoy: A TUN - SYR, F ION C A TUN - SYR, F EAS C A TUN - SYR. Hold A VEN to maintain DMZs with Austria, Germany, and France.

[F1902M] The Lepanto convoy was a complete success! My army has landed in Syria, perfectly outflanking Turkey's defenses. Austria and Russia coordinated flawlessly to attack Bulgaria, but Turkey's entrenched defense (supported by the Black Sea and Constantinople) held strong, resulting in a bounce. 

While Turkey survived this year without losing a center, their position is now crumbling from behind. Austria successfully took Greece and will build, which I must monitor closely to ensure I don't fall behind in our alliance. I need to secure Smyrna or another Turkish center for myself in 1903 to keep pace with Austrian growth.

In the west, France took London, and their war with Germany continues, keeping my western flank completely secure. My immediate goal for 1903 is to leverage my Syrian army to crack Turkey's defenses, likely targeting Smyrna, while maintaining my strong alliance with Austria and Russia.

[S1903M] Coordinated a massive strike against Turkey with Austria and Russia. Austria will attack BUL with 4 units (A SER supported by F AEG, F GRE, and Russian F RUM), while Russia also moves A SEV - ARM. I will attack SMY with A SYR supported by F EAS. I have successfully deceived Turkey into believing I am holding A SYR and F EAS while moving F ION west, prompting them to promise to hold F SMY and A CON. Meanwhile, Germany and England have strongly encouraged me to attack France's exposed southern flank. I have agreed with Germany to move F ION - TYS to begin this western pivot, while falsely assuring France that our DMZs remain intact.
Intent: Order A SYR - SMY supported by F EAS to secure a Turkish center. Order F ION - TYS to begin the naval shift against France. Order A VEN to Hold to maintain northern DMZs.

[S1903M] The Spring 1903 phase was a resounding success. The coordinated Austro-Russian-Italian strike completely shattered Turkish defenses. I successfully captured Smyrna with my Syrian army, while Austria took Bulgaria with Russian support. Turkey is now crippled and effectively eliminated as a threat. 

Simultaneously, my western pivot has begun flawlessly. F ION moved to the Tyrrhenian Sea without raising alarms. France is heavily distracted: they just lost London back to England and are locked in a fierce struggle with Germany over Burgundy. Their southern flank remains completely exposed to my impending naval assault.

While the Lepanto alliance has yielded great spoils, Austria's rapid growth (now holding Greece, Serbia, and Bulgaria) remains a long-term concern. My immediate goals for the Fall are to secure Smyrna, finalize the destruction of Turkey, and launch a surprise attack on France's Mediterranean centers while they are bogged down in the north.

[F1903M] Austria is holding BUL with support from AEG and GRE. Russia is attacking ANK with ARM and BLA with RUM. Turkey leaked to Russia that they are ordering CON-BUL supported by BLA. I agreed with Austria and Russia to hold SMY supported by EAS to guarantee the build. In the West, I have successfully deceived France into believing I will order TYS-TUN and VEN Hold, keeping their southern flank empty. Meanwhile, I have coordinated with England and Germany to launch a surprise attack on France: I will order TYS-LYO and VEN-PIE while Germany attacks BUR.
Intent: Order A SMY Hold supported by F EAS to secure the center for a build. Order F TYS - LYO and A VEN - PIE to launch a surprise attack on France's exposed southern flank, fulfilling my agreements with England and Germany while betraying France.

[F1903M] The Fall 1903 phase was a spectacular success. My surprise attack on France executed flawlessly; F TYS moved to LYO and A VEN to PIE without resistance. France was entirely distracted by their wars with Germany and England, even ordering A MAR to support BUR. Their southern flank is now completely exposed, and I am perfectly positioned to strike Marseilles or Spain next year. 

In the East, the Austro-Russian-Italian alliance held firm. I secured Smyrna, while Russia successfully captured Ankara. Turkey is now reduced to a single center (Constantinople) and faces imminent elimination. 

With 5 supply centers, I have earned a crucial build. I will likely build a fleet in Naples to accelerate my western naval campaign against France, though I must remain vigilant of Austria's growing power (now at 6 SCs). My immediate goals for 1904 are to capture French centers, finish Turkey, and maintain my alliances.

[S1904M] Austria and Russia are coordinating to eliminate Turkey by attacking CON (Austria's A BUL supported by F AEG and Russia's A ANK). I have agreed with both to hold A SMY supported by F EAS to secure my flank without interfering. Austria claims A TRI is moving to SER and promises the Venice/Adriatic DMZ remains intact, though France warned me Austria intends to attack Venice. In the West, I have coordinated a massive strike on France: Germany is attacking BUR (MUN-BUR S RUH) to cut support, England is attacking ENG and IRI, and I am attacking MAR with PIE supported by LYO. I am also moving my new fleet NAP to TYS to reinforce the assault.
Intent: Order A PIE - MAR supported by F LYO to capture the French center. Order F NAP - TYS to reinforce the western naval campaign. Order A SMY Hold supported by F EAS to secure the Turkish spoils while Austria and Russia finish off CON.

[S1904M] Austria executed a devastating stab, capturing Venice and moving into the Ionian Sea. France warned me of this exact move, but I foolishly trusted Austria's promise to move to Serbia. This massive betrayal completely shifts my strategic focus. 

Fortunately, my western campaign succeeded. With Germany cutting Burgundy, I captured Marseilles, and F NAP moved to TYS. 

However, my home centers are now in grave danger. Austria is my primary enemy. I must immediately pivot to defend Rome and Naples from the Ionian and Venice. I need to urgently contact Russia to coordinate a two-front war against Austria, as Russia is strong and expanding. I must also stabilize the West, relying on Germany and England to finish France so I can redirect my forces eastward. 

Lesson learned: never leave home centers exposed to a rapidly growing ally, regardless of past trustworthiness.

[F1904M] Austria stabbed me by taking Venice and the Ionian Sea, though they claim they will move A VEN - TYR and F ION - AEG this turn to establish a truce. I do not trust them and have formed a coalition with Russia to counterattack. Russia will attack CON and BUL, and I have agreed to support their A ANK - CON with my A SMY. I agreed to a truce with France, promising to hold MAR and not advance further west, allowing me to pivot F LYO east to TYS. Germany and England are continuing their assault on France (PAR and PIC) and Germany has promised to consider pressuring Austria's borders next year.
Intent: Order A SMY S Russian A ANK - CON to help Russia dismantle Austria's eastern flank. Order F TYS - ION supported by F EAS to reclaim the Ionian Sea. Order F LYO - TYS to reinforce the homeland against Austria. Order A MAR Hold to maintain the center and honor the truce with France.

[F1904M] Austria lied again, holding Venice and attacking the Tyrrhenian Sea instead of retreating as promised. Fortunately, I anticipated their treachery and successfully forced my fleet into the Ionian Sea with Eastern Mediterranean support, while my Lyon fleet moved to the Tyrrhenian to secure my homeland. 

The Russian coalition is working perfectly. My Smyrna army supported Russia's attack on Constantinople, successfully dislodging the Austrian army. With Russia also capturing Bulgaria, Austria's eastern empire is rapidly crumbling under our combined assault. 

In the West, I successfully held Marseilles, maintaining my foothold while honoring the truce with France. 

Moving forward, I must continue pressing Austria to punish their stab. Retaking Venice and pushing into the Aegean or Greece are my top priorities. I will maintain my strong alliance with Russia to dismantle Austria completely, while relying on England and Germany to keep France contained.

[S1905M] Austria proposed a ceasefire, promising to vacate Venice (to TYR) and Naples (to APU) if I move F ION to EAS and F TYS to LYO. I agreed to deceive them, but I plan to attack Naples. Russia is moving armies to Galicia, Ukraine, and Romania to crush Austria from the north, while holding CON and BUL/EC. England and Germany are focused on finishing France (Paris/Brest) and declined to attack Austria this turn, but asked me to hold MAR. France also agreed to a truce around MAR.
Intent: Order F TYS S F ION - NAP to forcefully retake Naples, ignoring the fake truce with Austria. Order A MAR Hold to honor agreements with France, Germany, and England while keeping the center secure. Order A SMY Hold to protect Russia's flank and maintain my eastern foothold.

[S1905M] [S1905M] My deception worked perfectly: I successfully retook Naples by force while Austria foolishly expected a truce. However, the naval war is far from over. Austria moved F AEG into the Ionian Sea and F TRI to the Adriatic, maintaining a dangerous presence right on my shores. Venice also remains in Austrian hands. 

Russia continues to be a reliable ally, successfully moving into Romania and Ukraine, though they bounced Austria in Galicia. With Russia's mounting pressure from the east and north, Austria's defenses should soon crack. 

In the west, France surprisingly supported my hold in Marseilles, honoring our truce even as Germany successfully captured Paris. France is rapidly collapsing, which secures my western flank for now. 

My immediate priority is to defend Rome, clear the Ionian Sea, and coordinate with Russia to finally break Austria's hold on Venice and the Balkans. I must not let Austria slip out of this trap.

[F1905M] England warned about Russia's rapid growth and urged a truce with Austria, but understood my need to secure my home centers first and agreed to pressure Austria to retreat. France and Germany both agreed to my holding Marseilles while they finish the western war. Russia and I are coordinating a massive strike on Austria: I will hold Naples (supported by TYS) to secure the center, while Russia attacks Galicia and Budapest. I established a fake defensive truce with Austria to ensure he doesn't attack Naples, telling him to focus entirely on Russia.
Intent: Order F NAP Hold and F TYS S F NAP to guarantee I secure the center against any Austrian attacks from ION or APU. Order A MAR Hold to maintain the truce with France and agreements with Germany/England. Order A SMY Hold to protect my eastern foothold and Russia's flank.

[F1905M] My defensive strategy worked perfectly. I successfully held Naples, officially reclaiming the center this winter. Austria formed a massive defensive shell in the south, cross-supporting the Ionian Sea and Apulia with all available units. However, this turtling allowed Russia to break through their northern lines, successfully dislodging the Austrian army in Galicia. Austria's collapse is now accelerating.

In the west, England and Germany captured Brest, further dismantling France while my hold on Marseilles remains secure and uncontested. 

With Naples secured, my immediate priority is breaking Austria's naval wall (ION/ADR/APU). I will need to build a new fleet in Rome or Naples to force my way back into the Ionian Sea. I must continue coordinating closely with Russia to squeeze Austria from both sides, break their defensive lines, and finally reclaim Venice.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Austria: Agreed to DMZ Galicia; I informed them of my WAR-UKR move. England: Agreed to a northern non-aggression pact where England takes Norway and I take Sweden (STP-BOT). Germany: Agreed to DMZ Prussia and Silesia; Germany supports my claim to Sweden. Turkey: After some confusion, we finalized a DMZ in the Black Sea; Turkey will move ANK-CON and I will move SEV-RUM. Italy & France: Friendly introductory talks, keeping options open for future coordination.
Intent: I will fulfill my negotiated agreements: F STP/SC - BOT (aiming for Sweden), A WAR - UKR (respecting Galicia DMZ), and F SEV - RUM (respecting BLA DMZ). I will also order A MOS - SEV to provide backup in the south and prepare for Fall.

[S1901M] Turkey blatantly betrayed our Black Sea DMZ agreement by moving F ANK - BLA, while I kept my word and moved F SEV - RUM. This immediately marks Turkey as untrustworthy and hostile. Fortunately, my A MOS - SEV move succeeded, providing crucial defense for Sevastopol against their fleet. 

On a positive note, my other neighbors proved reliable. Austria respected the Galicia DMZ, Germany honored the Prussia/Silesia DMZ, and England followed through on our northern non-aggression pact. 

My strategic position in the north is secure, and I am poised to take Sweden with F BOT. In the south, however, I must pivot to an anti-Turkish stance. I need to solidify an alliance with Austria to counter Turkey's aggression, ensure I secure Romania this fall, and coordinate a defense or counterattack against the Turkish fleet in the Black Sea.

[F1901M] Austria and Italy are fully committed to an anti-Turkish alliance, with Italy executing a Lepanto and Austria taking Greece. Austria warned me that Turkey confided plans to attack RUM with A BUL supported by F BLA. Turkey claims they are moving F BLA back to ANK to apologize for their Spring lie, but I am treating this as deception. England and Germany are honoring our northern agreements, leaving Sweden open for me while they take Norway and Denmark/Holland respectively.
Intent: I will fulfill my northern agreements by ordering F BOT - SWE. In the south, I will heavily defend Romania against the anticipated Turkish attack by ordering F RUM to hold, supported by both A SEV and A UKR.

[F1901M] Turkey's deception was fully exposed this phase. As Austria warned, Turkey attacked Romania with A BUL supported by F BLA. Fortunately, my heavy defense—supported by both Sevastopol and Ukraine—held firm, securing Romania. Austria's accurate intelligence cements them as a valuable and trustworthy ally. Italy also successfully executed their Lepanto opening by taking Tunis, setting the stage for our joint anti-Turkish campaign in 1902.

In the north, England and Germany honored our agreements, allowing me to take Sweden without contest. I now hold 6 supply centers and will gain two builds. 

My strategic position is excellent. I will use my builds to reinforce the southern front against Turkey, likely adding a fleet in Sevastopol to challenge the Black Sea, and an army to support the Austrian push. I must maintain my peaceful northern borders while focusing entirely on dismantling Turkey.

[S1902M] Austria (Ally) shared Turkey's exact orders (BUL-RUM, BLA S BUL-RUM, CON-BUL, SMY-CON) and is moving SER-BUL and GRE-AEG to disrupt them; we agreed I will heavily defend RUM and keep GAL empty. Italy (Ally) is executing a Lepanto (ION-EAS, NAP-ION). Turkey (Enemy) agreed to a fake truce (BLA-ANK, BUL H) which I am ignoring. France (Friendly) warned me that England (Unfriendly) is launching a massive naval attack on Scandinavia (EDI-NWG, LON-NTH, NTH-SKA) and is moving to the English Channel to assist me. Germany (Friendly) is forming a defensive wall against England (KIE-HEL, DEN H) and I agreed to hold SWE.
Intent: Heavily defend Romania against Turkey's 2-unit attack: F RUM H, A UKR S F RUM, A SEV S F RUM. Prepare defenses against England's incoming fleets: A MOS - STP. Maintain the defensive line with Germany: F SWE H. Respect the Galicia DMZ with Austria: A WAR - LVN.

[S1902M] Spring 1902 results confirm the strength and reliability of the Austro-Italian-Russian alliance. Austria's intelligence was perfectly accurate: Turkey attacked Romania with Black Sea support, but my heavy three-unit defense held flawlessly. Austria and Italy successfully executed their naval maneuvers into the Aegean and Eastern Mediterranean, effectively bottling up Turkey. I will continue to trust and coordinate with them to dismantle the Turkish threat.

In the north, France's warning about England was entirely correct. England has launched a massive naval invasion, moving fleets to the Barents Sea, Norwegian Sea, and Skagerrak. St. Petersburg and Sweden are now under severe threat. Fortunately, my preemptive move of an army to St. Petersburg succeeded, providing crucial defense. France's move to the English Channel is a welcome distraction for England. 

My strategic priority must now balance surviving England's northern assault while maintaining the southern push. I need to coordinate with Germany to defend Scandinavia and encourage France's anti-English campaign.

[F1902M] Austria and I agreed to a coordinated attack on Bulgaria: I will order F RUM S A SER - BUL, while Austria orders A SER - BUL and F AEG S A SER - BUL. Italy is convoying A TUN - SYR to further crush Turkey. Turkey is panicking and believes I am holding RUM and SEV, stating he will order F BLA H and A BUL H. In the north, England agreed to a truce in Scandinavia to deal with France in the English Channel, and Germany confirmed England is retreating F SKA - NTH. France is attacking London. Germany is holding F DEN and F HEL to maintain our DMZ.
Intent: Execute the coordinated attack on Turkey by ordering F RUM S A SER - BUL. Defend against any potential Turkish desperation moves with A SEV S F RUM and A UKR S F RUM. In the north, honor the Scandinavian truce and defensive lines by ordering F SWE H, A STP H, and A LVN S A STP.

[F1902M] Fall 1902 results confirm the absolute strength of the Austro-Italian-Russian alliance, though our enemies remain stubborn. Austria and I perfectly executed our coordinated 3-unit attack on Bulgaria. Unfortunately, Turkey anticipated this and mounted a 3-unit defense, resulting in a bounce. However, Italy successfully convoyed an army into Syria, landing directly behind Turkish lines! This brilliant maneuver will surely break the Turkish stalemate next year.

In the north, England completely betrayed our Scandinavian truce by attacking St. Petersburg from the Barents Sea. Thankfully, my defensive hold kept the city secure. England's treachery will cost him dearly, as France successfully invaded and captured London this phase. 

My strategic position remains highly secure. I must continue to hold the line in the north against a now-crippled England while letting France dismantle him. In the south, I will coordinate with Austria and Italy to leverage our new Syrian foothold and finally crush Turkey.

[S1903M] Austria and Italy are fully committed to crushing Turkey. Austria will order A SER - BUL supported by F AEG and F GRE. Italy will order A SYR - SMY supported by F EAS. I have agreed to support Austria's attack with F RUM and move A SEV - ARM to pressure Turkey's eastern flank. Turkey believes I am holding RUM and moving A UKR - GAL, and claims they will order A BUL H, F BLA S A BUL, A CON S A BUL. In the north, England apologized for their previous attack, disbanded F BAR, and agreed to a strict NAP where they hold NWY and I hold SWE and STP. Germany and France are fighting each other; Germany agreed to maintain our DMZs, and I declined France's request to attack Germany or Norway to focus on Turkey.
Intent: Execute the coordinated attack on Turkey by ordering F RUM S A SER - BUL and A SEV - ARM. Move A UKR - SEV to backfill and prepare for future southern operations. In the north, honor the NAP with England and DMZs with Germany by ordering F SWE H, A STP H, and A LVN H.

[S1903M] The Austro-Italian-Russian alliance executed flawlessly this spring. Austria captured Bulgaria with my support from Romania, and Italy successfully landed in Smyrna. Turkey's defensive lines are completely broken, and they will face severe disbandments this winter. My move to Armenia positions me perfectly to pressure Ankara or the Black Sea next phase, ensuring I get my share of the Turkish spoils.

In the north, England honored our non-aggression pact, holding Norway while successfully retaking London from France. Germany and France remain locked in a border war, with Germany surprisingly supporting English control of Belgium. 

My strategic position is highly secure, but Austria's rapid expansion in the Balkans (now holding Serbia, Greece, and Bulgaria) is a growing concern. I must maintain our alliance to finish off Turkey, but I need to remain vigilant regarding Galicia and Romania. My immediate priority is securing Ankara for myself before my allies consume the rest of the board.

[F1903M] Austria and Italy are holding their newly acquired Turkish centers (BUL and SMY) with heavy support. Turkey has been deceived into believing I will hold my units, and they have confirmed they will order A CON - BUL supported by F BLA. This leaves the Black Sea and Ankara vulnerable. In the north, England and Germany have both agreed to maintain our NAPs and DMZs, keeping their units focused on their war with France. France requested I attack England or Germany, but I declined to focus on Turkey.
Intent: Execute the final strike on Turkey by ordering A ARM - ANK and F RUM - BLA (supported by A SEV). Since Turkey's F BLA is busy supporting an attack on Bulgaria, my attack on BLA will succeed and dislodge their fleet, while ARM walks into Ankara. In the north, honor the NAPs and DMZs by ordering F SWE Hold, A STP Hold, and A LVN Hold.

[F1903M] The Austro-Italian-Russian alliance has effectively neutralized Turkey. I successfully captured Ankara, reducing Turkey to a single supply center (Constantinople). My attempt to take the Black Sea (F RUM - BLA) bounced because Turkey played defensively, ordering F BLA to support A CON instead of attacking Bulgaria as they had claimed. Regardless, their defensive lines are shattered, and they will face disbandments.

In the north, the situation remains stable. England and Germany honored our non-aggression pacts and demilitarized zones, focusing entirely on their conflict with France. 

My strategic position is excellent. I will gain a build this winter, bringing me to 7 supply centers. Italy is already shifting units west (to Piedmont and Gulf of Lyon), signaling trust in our southern coalition. My immediate priority is to secure the Black Sea, eliminate Turkey's final center, and carefully manage my relationship with a growing Austria as we transition into the mid-game.

[S1904M] Austria and I agreed to eliminate Turkey; I promised to support his A BUL - CON with A ANK, while moving F SEV to BLA. Turkey begged for my A ANK to support his A CON to hold, which I agreed to in messages but intend to betray. Italy is holding SMY/EAS and focusing on France. France is facing a 3v1 (E/G/I) and begged for help; I promised to shift northern forces to pressure England. England and Germany reaffirmed our NAPs/DMZs, but I plan to break the NAP with England to assist France.
Intent: Eliminate Turkey by ordering A ANK S A BUL - CON. Secure the Black Sea with F SEV - BLA and F RUM S F SEV - BLA. Hold A ARM. In the north, break the NAP with England to relieve France by ordering F SWE - NWY and A STP - FIN. Maintain the DMZ with Germany by holding A LVN.

[S1904M] Spring 1904 went exactly to plan on my end, but the global board state has shifted dramatically. I successfully supported Austria into Constantinople, finally eliminating Turkey, and secured the Black Sea. In the north, I executed my planned stab on England, capturing Norway to relieve pressure on France. 

However, France is collapsing faster than expected, losing Marseilles to Italy and Burgundy to Germany. The biggest shock of the season was Austria stabbing Italy by walking into Venice! Austria's explosive growth—taking both Constantinople and Venice this turn—makes them the most dangerous power on the board. 

With Turkey gone, my southeastern flank is secure, but Austria's unchecked expansion is a massive looming threat. I must fortify Galicia and Romania immediately. In the north, I need to decide whether to continue pressing a distracted England or consolidate my forces to prepare for an inevitable clash with Austria. Germany remains a reliable buffer for now.

[F1904M] Deceived Austria into believing I will support their hold in CON and maintain borders. Coordinated a massive stab on Austria with Italy: Italy will order A SMY S A ANK - CON and attack ION, while I attack CON and BUL. Agreed to a truce with England, promising to hold NWY and stay out of NTH/NWG while they retreat to home centers. Reaffirmed strict DMZs with Germany. Ignored France's plea to spare Austria (France fears Italy will focus entirely on them if Austria falls).
Intent: Execute a surprise stab on Austria. Order A ANK - CON supported by F BLA (and Italy's A SMY). Order F RUM - BUL/EC to steal an empty center. Move A ARM - SEV to reposition. In the north, honor the truce with England and DMZs with Germany by holding F NWY, A STP, and A LVN.

[F1904M] The coordinated stab on Austria was a resounding success. With Italy's crucial support from Smyrna, I captured Constantinople and slipped into Bulgaria, while Italy successfully dislodged the Austrian fleet in the Ionian Sea. Austria's explosive growth has been halted, and they are now reeling on multiple fronts. 

However, my northern flank has collapsed. England blatantly betrayed our truce, launching a coordinated attack to dislodge my fleet in Norway. Their word is entirely worthless, and I must treat them as a permanent hostile threat. Germany, thankfully, continues to respect our demilitarized zones.

Despite the loss of Norway, my southern gains will yield a net positive in builds. My immediate priority is to continue dismantling Austria alongside Italy before they can recover, while urgently fortifying St. Petersburg and Scandinavia against further English aggression. I must balance the southern offensive with a staunch northern defense.

[S1905M] Agreed to a 'truce' with Austria where they focus west on Italy while I secure BUL/CON/RUM/GAL. However, I am secretly coordinating with Italy to crush Austria; Italy will attack Naples while I march armies into Galicia, Ukraine, and Romania to apply massive northern pressure. Reinstated a strict NAP with England (I hold SWE/STP, they hold NWY/NWG) and reaffirmed DMZs with Germany (Prussia, Silesia, Baltic Sea). Declined France's pleas for help to focus on the southern offensive.
Intent: Move A WAR - GAL, A MOS - UKR, and A SEV - RUM to secure the border and prepare for a massive ground offensive against Austria. Hold F BUL/EC, A CON, and F BLA to lock down the southeast. Hold A STP, F SWE, and A LVN to honor the northern NAP with England and DMZs with Germany.

[S1905M] The Spring 1905 phase yielded mixed but overall positive results. My attempt to slip into Galicia from Warsaw was thwarted by a supported Austrian move (VIE-GAL), establishing an annoying Austrian presence on my border. However, my deployments to Ukraine and Romania succeeded, creating a solid wall of armies (WAR, UKR, RUM) ready to dislodge them in the Fall. 

Crucially, my secret collaboration with Italy was a massive success. While I applied northern pressure, Italy successfully captured Naples, dislodging the Austrian fleet. Austria is now bleeding centers and fighting a losing two-front war. 

In the north, both England and Germany honored our NAPs and DMZs. They are entirely focused on dismantling France, with Germany successfully taking Paris this phase. My strategy remains clear: maintain the peaceful northern buffer while coordinating with Italy to crush Austria. Next phase, I must force Austria out of Galicia.

[F1905M] Successfully deceived Austria into believing a 'truce' is in place where both sides hold their borders (WAR, UKR, RUM for Russia; GAL, BUD for Austria). Secretly coordinated with Italy to launch a massive offensive against Austria: Italy will secure Naples while I attack Galicia and Budapest. Reaffirmed strict NAPs and DMZs with England (Scandinavia) and Germany (Prussia, Silesia, Baltic Sea), who are both focused on eliminating France.
Intent: Betray the truce with Austria and launch a coordinated attack: A WAR - GAL supported by A UKR, and A RUM - BUD (to cut potential support for Galicia). Hold A STP, F SWE, and A LVN to honor northern agreements with England and Germany. Hold F BLA, F BUL/EC, and A CON to secure southern gains.

[F1905M] [F1905M] The Fall phase was a resounding tactical success. My coordinated strike against Austria worked perfectly: Romania attacked Budapest to cut its support, allowing Warsaw and Ukraine to successfully dislodge the Austrian army in Galicia. This secures my border and positions me perfectly to assault Vienna and Budapest next year. 

Crucially, Italy successfully held Naples, meaning Austria will lose the center and be forced to disband a unit (likely the dislodged army in Galicia). The Austro-Italian war continues to drain Austrian resources while I advance from the north and east.

In the north, England and Germany honored our NAPs and DMZs, focusing entirely on their joint campaign to eliminate France (successfully taking Brest). I will maintain this peaceful northern buffer. My strategy remains unchanged: hold the line in Scandinavia and St. Petersburg while relentlessly pushing into the Austrian core (Budapest, Vienna, Serbia) alongside Italy.
